title: Lorenzini's cyclic sandpile group problem
statement_text: How often is the sandpile group $S(G)$ cyclic, that is $\mu(G) = 1$?
context_text: Lorenzini [33] raised the question of how frequently the sandpile group of a graph is cyclic. This motivates the paper's analysis of the minimum number of generators of the sandpile group of polygon flowers, culminating in a necessary and sufficient condition for cyclicity.
paper_contribution: Proves a necessary and sufficient condition for $S(F)$ to be cyclic for any polygon flower $F = (C_t; P_1, \ldots, P_t)$, expressed in terms of arithmetic properties of the spanning tree counts $\tau(P_i)$ and $\tau(P_i/e_i)$.
notes: Mentioned in introduction prose without a labelled theorem environment; attributed to reference [33]. PDF source — math may be garbled. The paper addresses a special case (polygon flowers) of this general question.
paper_title: The sandpile group of a polygon flower
